chunk_summary: From 12–24 months, toddlers should eat modified family foods with dietary diversity, portion control, and safety measures while continuing breastfeeding on demand.
learning_objectives: Understand appropriate portion sizes and meal frequency for toddlers aged 12–24 months.
Learn strategies to ensure dietary diversity, including vegetarian adaptations.
Identify foods and practices to avoid for safety and nutritional balance.
Recognize the importance of caregiver involvement in mealtime structure and self-feeding.
key_facts: Toddlers should have 3 family meals and 2 planned snacks daily due to small stomach capacity.
Portion sizes are typically three-quarters to one small bowl per meal, varying with appetite and health.
Aim for at least 4 food groups daily: grains/tubers, pulses/nuts, dairy, eggs or flesh foods, and fruits/vegetables.
Limit cow’s milk to 400–500 mL/day to prevent iron deficiency and anemia.
Avoid choking hazards like whole nuts, grapes, and hard raw vegetables unless modified.
reference_quote: From 12–24 months, the child should largely eat family foods modified for safety, while breastfeeding can continue on demand. (UNICEF)
document_key_topics: Dietary diversity and portion control for toddlers
Safety measures and choking prevention in toddler feeding
